data.title: Uranium Royalty taps Andy Marshall as CFO
data.description:
• Uranium Royalty (NASDAQ:UROY) has tapped Andy Marshall as chief financial officer, succeeding Josephine Man in such position.
  • Marshall is a chartered accountant and chartered financial analyst with over 20 years of senior financial leadership experience in the natural resources sector.
  • UROY --1.15% premarket to $2.56
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Earnings Snapshot: Exxon Mobil reports Q2 estimates beat, record Permian production
Earnings Snapshot: Chevron beats Q2 profit estimates, reports record production
Butterfly Network reports mixed Q2 results; updates FY25 outlook, announces CFO transition
data.description:
• Butterfly Network press release (NYSE:BFLY): Q2 Non-GAAP EPS of -$0.03 beats by $0.02.
  • Revenue of $23.4M (+8.9% Y/Y) misses by $0.32M.

  • Cash and cash equivalents: Cash and cash equivalents were $148.1 million as of June 30, 2025.

    Guidance

    Revised Revenue guidance and improved Adjusted EBITDA guidance for the Fiscal Year 2025:

    • Revenue of $92 million to $96 million or approximately 15% growth (prior $96 million to $100 million or approximately 20% growth) vs. $97.23M consensus.
    • Adjusted EBITDA loss of $32 million - $37 million.

    Chief Financial and Operations Officer Transition

    Effective today, August 1, 2025, Heather Getz, Chief Financial and Operations Officer, has resigned to pursue other interests. Ms. Getz will assist the Company to ensure a successful transition of her responsibilities prior to her departure.

data.title: Apple rises after 'solid' Q3, as Wedbush says iPhone China-beat 'star of the show'
data.description:
Wedbush said solid results from Apple (NASDAQ:AAPL), with an impressive iPhone beat in China, was the star of the show, following the U.S. tech giant's quarterly results post-market on Thursday.

The firm maintained its Outperform rating on Apple with a $270 price target on the stock. Shares of Apple rose about 2% premarket on Friday.

"Apple announced its FY3Q25 ((June)) results which came in much better than expected featuring strong top and bottom-lines led by iPhone and Service particularly in China which return to positive growth (up 4% y/y) which is a key theme for Apple as this core region has been a persistent growth headwind over the past year," said analysts led by Daniel Ives.

The analysts noted that Apple reported total revenues of $94.04B which came in well ahead of the Street's estimate of $89.35B estimate with double-digit growth across iPhone, Mac and Services led by the all-important iPhone revenues of $44.58B which was well above the Street’s expectation of $40.29B as  the company saw significant improvements in the key China region heading into the iPhone 17 product cycle over the next year.

"This was a major step in the right direction for Cook and Cupertino with China the star of the show," said the analysts.

Ives and his team said that China, importantly, delivered $15.37B in revenue, (up 4% year-over-year) as the key region was considerably better than both Wedbush and the Street’s expectations by returning to positive growth as government stimulus helped stimulate demand during the quarter.

In another research note, Wedbush said that the company gave solid September directional guidance.

Ives and his team said the company expects revenue to grow mid to high single digits with Services growth similar to the June quarter's year-over-year growth rate in September. This was above the Street and a good sign around overall demand especially in China heading into the iPhone 17 release in September.

Apple continues to focus on production in India as the majority of iPhones sold in the U.S. are now being made in India rather than in China given the complex Trump tariff backdrop that Apple is navigating, according to the analysts.

"Right now tariffs are a problem for Apple with an expected headwind of roughly $1 billion in the September quarter," said the Ives and his team.

In addition, the analysts said that while Apple is expanding its AI investments internally, the reality is it is not moving the needle and patience is wearing thin with investors.

 "It's clear to us the innovations around AI are not going to come from Apple Park...it has to be external partnerships and significant M&A looking ahead. In our view Apple needs to acquire Perplexity to truly catalyze its AI strategy among other options," the analysts added.

Update: The story was updated with additional information from a new Wedbush note.

data.title: Melco Resorts' new casino in Sri Lanka could shake up the South Asia gaming scene
data.description:
Melco Resorts & Entertainment (NASDAQ:MLCO) will open a high-profile casino in Sri Lanka on August 2 inside the City of Dreams Sri Lanka integrated resort. The company partnered with John Keells Holdings PLC on the project, which is Colombo's first fully integrated luxury resort and casino.

Currently, Sri Lanka has five established casinos, all located in Colombo. The existing casinos in the nation are Bally's Casino, Bellagio Colombo, Casino Marina Colombo, Star Dust Casino, and The Ritz Club Sri Lanka.

The highly anticipated City of Dreams project is notable because it represents Sri Lanka's largest private sector investment in luxury hospitality, with an investment total of over $1.2 billion. The casino itself has received an investment of $125 million from Melco Resorts (NASDAQ:MLCO) and is situated within the Cinnamon Life complex, one of John Keells’ flagship developments.

City of Dreams Sri Lanka will feature extensive accommodation with a total of 800 rooms and suites. Of those, 687 are operated under the Cinnamon Life brand, already in service since 2024, and 113 ultra-luxury rooms sit atop the tower under Melco's (MLCO) exclusive Nüwa brand.

 

 

 

The centerpiece of the project will be the casino, which is the largest in Sri Lanka and among the most expansive in South Asia, covering 180,000 square feet. Melco (MLCO) has secured a 20-year casino license beginning in April 2024, positioning it as the sole international gaming operator in the country for years to come. Beyond gaming, the integrated resort includes a luxury shopping mall, cutting-edge facilities for meetings and events, a collection of restaurants led by renowned chefs, and numerous venues for performances and international cultural events.

Melco Resorts & Entertainment (MLCO) CEO Lawrence Ho said the company believes Sri Lanka has immense potential and thinks the opportunity complements the existing portfolio of properties. "City of Dreams Sri Lanka is expected to serve as a catalyst for stimulating tourism demand and promoting economic growth in Sri Lanka, drawing inspiration from the successful examples set by similar integrated resorts in other jurisdictions," he highlighted.

Looking ahead, analysts have offered mixed opinions on how the Asian market will react to the new gambling destination. Some have pointed to the rapidly expanding number of outbound travelers from India that could head to the destination, while others warn of a tepid response. "This is going to be a destination market, which requires travel from outside and stay overnight," stated Seaport Research Partners analyst Vitaly Umansky. "And it is not going to be like the unique market of Vegas, where the gaming component is smaller and accounts for 40% revenue than the nongaming component, which earns 60% revenue," he added.
